channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: A Trump Endorsement Falls Flat: 4 Election Takeaways From Iowa and Beyond
item.description: The president’s pick for governor of Iowa lost his primary, while Democrats in the state chose their nominee in what they hope will be a competitive Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:description: Representative Randy Feenstra, a Republican, lost his primary bid for governor of Iowa despite a late endorsement from President Trump.

item.title: 5 Takeaways From the California Primary Election
item.description: Without a dominant candidate in the governor’s race, Democratic voters ultimately wanted to ensure that their party wasn’t shut out of the general election.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Jennifer Medina
item.media:credit: Minh Connors for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kevin Stull, right, fills out a ballot as barber Bryan Diaz waits to give a haircut at Ana’s Hair Salon on Election Day in the Mission District of San Francisco.

item.title: Deb Haaland Wins Nomination in New Mexico Governor’s Race
item.description: Ms. Haaland, a former Interior secretary, took a big step toward making history as the first Native American woman to be a governor when she won the primary in her Democratic state.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault
item.media:credit: Nina Riggio for The New York Times
item.media:description: Deb Haaland, former secretary for the Interior Department, spoke to a group gathered at a Veterans Helping Veterans building in Gallup, New Mexico, in April.

item.title: Trump’s Controversial Pick to Oversee U.S. Intelligence, and a Surge in Preventable Diseases
item.description: Plus, how Marcia Lucas was “the heart” behind “Star Wars.”
item.dc:creator: Tracy Mumford, Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, David E. Sanger and Sheera Frenkel
item.media:description: Bill Pulte, a Trump loyalist who leads the federal housing finance agency, will oversee the U.S. intelligence agencies.

item.title: War Games and Warnings on Strait of Hormuz Went Unheeded by Trump
item.description: Over the past two decades, Iran repeatedly threatened to close down the waterway. President Trump underestimated Iran’s ability to do so.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz has become a huge source of leverage in negotiations with President Trump.

item.title: Order Shielding Trump Family From I.R.S. Audits Will Remain, Blanche Says
item.description: The acting attorney general said the administration was preserving a broad order protecting the president and his family from audits of already filed returns, despite dropping a $1.8 billion payout fund.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times

item.title: Justice Dept. Won’t Proceed With Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund, Blanche Says
item.description: The acting attorney general told lawmakers that he would leave in place an order forcing the I.R.S. to drop investigations into President Trump, his family and his businesses.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, made clear to a House committee on Tuesday that the fund proposal would be permanently withdrawn.

item.title: G.O.P. Works to Jump-Start Immigration Bill After Trump Retreat on Fund
item.description: Republicans hoped the acting attorney general’s vow that the administration was “not moving forward” with a fund for people claiming to be victimized by the government would unlock the votes.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni and Robert Jimison
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator John Thune, the majority leader, at a news conference on Tuesday.

item.title: New Intelligence Role Puts Bill Pulte’s Housing Agenda in Doubt
item.description: Mr. Pulte has had difficulty boosting the housing market as head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency. Now he will also serve as acting intelligence director.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Goldstein and Lydia DePillis
item.category: Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corp (Freddie Mac)
Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:description: Bill Pulte, the director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, had some early success in bringing down mortgage rates, but housing prices have remained high.

item.title: Trump Aims New Tariffs at 59 Countries and the European Union
item.description: Trump administration officials said they planned to impose levies of up to 12.5 percent on countries that failed to crack down on goods made with forced labor.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.media:description: Cotton being loaded in Xinjiang, China, in 2021.

item.title: Trump Endorses Abelardo De La Espriella, Right-Wing Presidential Candidate in Colombia
item.description: Mr. Trump said that the candidate, Abelardo De La Espriella, was important for the U.S.-Colombia relationship and called his left-wing rival a “Radical Left Marxist.”
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Abelardo De La Espriella at his closing rally in Medellín, Colombia. He gave campaign speeches from a bulletproof booth.

item.title: Rubio Suggests U.S. Return to Global Vaccine Program in Rebuke of Kennedy
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated in pointed testimony to senators that he was reclaiming control of the U.S. relationship with Gavi, an international vaccine alliance.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nolen and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:description: The State Department is “going to be re-engaging on the issue of Gavi,” Secretary of State Marco Rubio said in a hearing on Tuesday.

item.title: Want Courtside Seats for the Knicks? Be Rich, Famous and a Die-Hard Fan.
item.description: Prime tickets on the floor for the N.B.A. Finals are coveted and very hard to get. The No. 1 requirement: You must love the Knicks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Maslin Nir
item.media:description: Timothée Chalamet, Kylie Jenner and Ben Stiller are courtside regulars at Knicks games.

item.title: N.B.A. Finals Introduce Music by Nicholas Britell and Nas
item.description: The league is rolling out music by the composer Nicholas Britell and the rapper Nas that, if successful, could become the sport’s sonic shorthand.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Barone
item.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sonic dream team: Nas, left, and Nicholas Britell in Brooklyn.

item.title: Knicks Fans Invade San Antonio, Chasing a Finals Rematch 27 Years in the Making
item.description: From Spike Lee to die-hard fans haunted by the 1999 finals, New Yorkers have descended on San Antonio in hopes of witnessing the franchise’s first championship in more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval and Sarah Maslin Nir
item.media:credit: Jordan Vonderhaar for The New York Times
item.media:description: Eric Tom, left, and his wife, Mary Anne, were among a slew of New York Knicks fans descending on San Antonio International Airport ahead of the 2026 N.B.A. finals.

item.title: CBS News Fires Scott Pelley of ‘60 Minutes’
item.description: Mr. Pelley, a former “CBS Evening News” anchor, was ousted after months of tensions between staff and Bari Weiss, the network’s editor in chief.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin and Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:description: Scott Pelley was fired a day after a tense meeting where he accused the CBS editor in chief, Bari Weiss, of “murdering ‘60 Minutes.’”

item.title: U.S. Is Said to Be Investigating George Santos Over Kalshi Betting
item.description: The former congressman was referred to federal authorities after he bet on his own attendance at the State of the Union address, a person familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany and Sharon LaFraniere
item.media:description: The inquiry into former Representative George Santos is the third insider trading case to come to light in recent weeks.

item.title: At Interlochen, Where Jeffrey Epstein’s Shadow Still Lingers
item.description: The arts school and camp is still contending with the fallout from its former ties to Mr. Epstein, an alumnus and donor accused of preying on two girls he met there.
item.dc:creator: Graham Bowley and Jamie Kelter Davis

item.title: The Big Questions About Jeffrey Epstein: What The Times Has Learned
item.description: Here is what we know about the sexual predator whose secrets spurred an international reckoning over money, power and complicity. Check back for updates.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Supreme Court Clears the Way for Republican-Friendly Map in Alabama
item.description: In the first major case since the justices narrowed the Voting Rights Act, the court cleared the way for Alabama to use a map preferred by Republicans that leaves the state with only one majority-Black district.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane
item.media:description: The Supreme Court ruled in 2023 that an Alabama congressional district map with only one majority-Black district diluted the power of Black voters.

item.title: Graham Platner Meets With Senate Democrats Amid Texting Scandal
item.description: Mr. Platner, the likely Democratic nominee for Senate in Maine, met in Washington with several senators. Chuck Schumer, the minority leader, dodged questions about his confidence in Mr. Platner.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:description: Graham Platner met with Democratic senators in Washington days after reports that he had sent sexual messages to women outside his marriage.

item.title: This Rugged New York Heron Is Suddenly Facing Extinction. Why?
item.description: Black-crowned night herons could go the way of the passenger pigeon, vanishing from the city. Why remains a mystery.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
item.media:description: They’re big, and they’re friendly (mostly). But black-crowned night herons and their chicks, pictured above, face perilous times.

item.title: The Toll of a ‘Missing Scientists’ Conspiracy Theory on the Families Left Behind
item.description: The claim that their loved ones’ deaths and disappearances are linked is almost certainly false — but the loss remains real.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Nazaryan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Rebecca Noble for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elizabeth Weiss, an anthropologist whose husband, Nick Pope, a prominent U.F.O. researcher, died in April. “If you start looking for patterns, you will find them,” she said, quoting him.

item.title: How Taylor Swift Writes a Song, In Her Own Words
item.description: The artist shares stories behind some of her biggest hits, her love of a “rant bridge” and how life in the public eye informs the stories she tells in her songs.

item.title: Spike Lee: Knicks in Six
item.description: The Knicks are New York’s soul, and they are bringing us together again in the championship series against San Antonio.
item.dc:creator: Spike Lee
item.media:credit: Corey Olsen for The New York Times

item.title: Feeling Nihilistic? You’re Not Alone.
item.description: A case for mourning the American dream.
item.dc:creator: Nadja Spiegelman, Tressie McMillan Cottom, Brock Colyar and Vishakha Darbha
item.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; photo by Mario Tama/Getty

item.title: Painted Into a Corner
item.description: We look at the state of the Iran war three months on.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: In the Strait of Hormuz on Monday.

item.title: Late Night Wants Trump to Give Peace Talks a Chance
item.description: “Oh, I’m sorry, is wrapping up the war you started not interesting enough?” Michael Kosta said on Tuesday, after the president called negotiations with Iran “boring.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Protests in UK Over Henry Nowak Murder Case Turn Violent, as Leaders Urge Calm
item.description: Eleven police officers were wounded as demonstrators pelted them with rocks and garbage cans, scenes a British cabinet minister called “unacceptable.”
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: Protesters and riot police officers in Southampton, a city in southern England, on Tuesday night.
